name: ASE (Atomic Simulation Environment)
pricing_notes: Open source (LGPL-2.1); DTU Physics / Jakob Schiøtz et al.

performance.notes: Wrapper for external calculators (DFT codes); own speed depends on calculator

agent_readiness.notes: Atomic structure manipulation and simulation environment. from ase import Atoms. from ase.build import bulk, molecule. Cu = bulk('Cu', 'fcc', a=3.6). H2O = molecule('H2O'). from ase.calculators.emt import EMT. H2O.calc = EMT(); print(H2O.get_potential_energy()). from ase.optimize import BFGS; opt = BFGS(atoms); opt.run(fmax=0.01). from ase.io import read, write; atoms = read('structure.xyz'). Visualization: view(atoms). Interfaces: VASP, GPAW, Quantum ESPRESSO, LAMMPS, etc. Databases: ase.db. ML potentials: via MACE, CHGNet.

gotchas: LGPL-2.1 — dynamic linking required for commercial use
ASE itself is a wrapper — actual DFT requires external codes (VASP, GPAW) which may be licensed
EMT calculator is only for testing — not accurate for production use
No type annotations — poor IDE support
best_when: Atomic structure manipulation (build, transform, visualize crystal/molecular structures)
Geometry optimization and MD with external DFT calculators (VASP, GPAW, QE)
Reading/writing 50+ structure file formats (CIF, POSCAR, XYZ, etc.)
Gluing together ML potentials (MACE, CHGNet) with structure optimization
avoid_when: Pure cheminformatics (use RDKit — more feature-rich for molecules)
Standalone quantum chemistry (needs external DFT code for accuracy)
Classical MD trajectories (use MDAnalysis for analysis)
summary: ASE provides atomic structure manipulation and simulation environment. bulk('Cu', 'fcc'). molecule('H2O'). atoms.calc = calculator. BFGS geometry optimization. read/write 50+ formats (CIF, POSCAR, XYZ). ASE database. Interfaces to VASP, GPAW, QE, LAMMPS, ML potentials. LGPL-2.1 by DTU. Central hub for computational materials science workflows. Needs external DFT code for production calculations.
